UC-001.Action: Calls MCP tool `search_messages` with query, `country_code`, and optional filters (without `filters.chat_ids`)
UC-001.Goal: Search tg-chat-rag corpus through MCP with deterministic proxy behavior scoped to a specific country
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: Request with nested `filters.chat_ids` is rejected; `country_code` is required and selects per-country `tg_chat_ids` from `country_settings` table; request without explicit chat_ids gets internal country-scoped chat scope injected

UC-002.Goal: Retrieve surrounding context for a specific message UID
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: Non-empty `message_uid` is required and upstream response is returned in deterministic MCP result envelope

UC-003.Goal: Retrieve semantically related messages for exploration workflows
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Non-empty `message_uid` is required and result is proxied without hidden business branching

UC-004.Goal: Resolve source metadata for message identifiers scoped to a specific country
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: `message_uids` is required, min=1, max=100; `country_code` is required; invalid payloads fail deterministically

UC-005.Action: Proxies supported tool calls to tg-chat-rag methods API
UC-005.Goal: Keep MCP surface thin and deterministic while delegating retrieval logic upstream
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: All upstream calls use `TG_CHAT_RAG_BEARER_TOKEN` and are made via `/api/v1/methods/<tool_name>`

UC-006.Action: Exposes only fixed allowlist of MCP tools
UC-006.Goal: Prevent accidental tool-surface drift and uncontrolled method passthrough
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: Only `search_messages`, `get_message_context`, `get_related_messages`, `list_sources` (proxied), and `get_site_sources`, `search_sites`, `get_page_chunk` (local) are registerable in runtime; unified cross-source search tool is not exposed

UC-007.Action: Authenticates against `/mcp` using FastMCP OAuth Proxy flow
UC-007.Goal: Use OAuth-based auth only for MCP transport surface
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated `/mcp` calls receive OAuth challenge/flow; successful OAuth session is required for tool execution

UC-008.Goal: Keep admin surface simple and restricted to root secret workflow
UC-008.AcceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated `/admin/*` access redirects to `/admin/login`; valid token creates admin session cookie

UC-009.Action: Enforces strict auth-path isolation between MCP and admin surfaces
UC-009.Goal: Avoid credential confusion and privilege crossover
UC-009.AcceptanceCriteria: `ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN` is never accepted on `/mcp`; OAuth session is not used as admin session

UC-010.Action: Opens public landing page and chooses to start testing
UC-010.Goal: Reach user portal with minimal friction from a shareable public URL
UC-010.AcceptanceCriteria: Landing exposes a single primary action that redirects to `/portal` without invite-link requirements

UC-011.Action: Opens custom `/portal/register` page and starts OAuth with an allowed social provider
UC-011.Goal: Create account without manual platform-admin intervention and without password flow
UC-011.AcceptanceCriteria: Registration page offers only social sign-in options; no email/password registration fields are shown

UC-012.Action: Opens custom `/portal/login` page and starts OAuth with an allowed social provider
UC-012.Goal: Access tester area that explains how to connect MCP in external AI agents
UC-012.AcceptanceCriteria: Login page offers only social sign-in options; no email/password login fields are shown

UC-013.Action: Auto-provisions MCP access entitlements for newly registered/first-login portal users
UC-013.Goal: Eliminate manual permission grants by platform admin for tester onboarding
UC-013.AcceptanceCriteria: After successful registration/login, user is granted required MCP access role/policy automatically or receives deterministic recoverable error

UC-014.Action: Reads MCP connection instructions in portal and configures external AI agent
UC-014.Goal: Achieve end-to-end flow from shared link to first successful MCP usage
UC-014.AcceptanceCriteria: Portal shows MCP endpoint/auth requirements and tester can execute at least one of the four active tools through authorized client flow

UC-015.Action: Enforces auth isolation across three surfaces (`/mcp`, `/admin/*`, `/portal/*`)
UC-015.Goal: Prevent privilege crossover between machine auth, operator auth, and tester portal auth
UC-015.AcceptanceCriteria: Portal session cannot access admin routes; admin session cannot bypass `/mcp` OAuth; MCP OAuth token is not treated as portal/admin session

UC-016.Action: Completes OAuth callback from Logto for portal social login and creates local portal session
UC-016.Goal: Finalize custom-UI social authentication flow with deterministic success/failure outcomes
UC-016.AcceptanceCriteria: `/portal/auth/callback` exchanges authorization code, handles expected OAuth errors safely, and redirects to `/portal/home` on success

UC-017.Action: Tracks per-user per-country MCP tool call counts in PostgreSQL during tool execution and exposes usage statistics to the portal
UC-017.Goal: Provide visibility into individual user MCP usage per country for portal display and future analytics
UC-017.AcceptanceCriteria: Each successful MCP tool call increments a per-user per-tool per-country counter in PostgreSQL with composite key `(user_id, tool_name, country_code)`; user identity is extracted from OAuth session JWT `sub` claim; portal home page displays per-tool call counts and total for the authenticated user; tracking failures never block tool execution

UC-018.Goal: Retrieve curated source registry metadata for a specific country for agent search orchestration without fetching page content
UC-018.AcceptanceCriteria: `get_site_sources` requires `country_code` parameter and returns `description_and_tiers` and active registry `sources` from `site_sources` table filtered by `country_code`; tool is metadata-only and never returns page content; tool requires OAuth authentication

UC-019.Action: Calls MCP tool `search_sites(query, country_code, top_k?, source_ids?)`
UC-019.Goal: Find relevant evidence snippets across curated travel websites for a specific country with strict attribution
UC-019.AcceptanceCriteria: `country_code` is required; response includes ranked hits with `source_id`, `tier`, `domain`, `original_page_url`, `snippet`, `chunk_id`, `score` filtered to sources belonging to the specified country; retrieval uses local index only and never fetches live pages in request path

UC-020.Goal: Retrieve bounded evidence excerpt for grounding without exposing full-page dumps
UC-020.AcceptanceCriteria: Response returns bounded `chunk_excerpt` with attribution fields (`source_id`, `original_page_url`, `title`) and optional neighbor excerpts; full page verbatim output is not allowed

UC-021.Action: Runs scheduled curated-site ingestion via Spider crawl and local indexing pipeline
UC-021.Goal: Maintain fresh searchable site index with deterministic cadence and crawl-only policy
UC-021.AcceptanceCriteria: Worker executes crawl-only ingestion (including targeted single-URL recrawl via crawl endpoint), respects per-source interval/page caps, and writes normalized pages/chunks/embeddings with active `index_version`

UC-022.Action: Starts API and worker as separate entrypoints
UC-022.Goal: Isolate interactive MCP/UI runtime from long-running crawl/index jobs
UC-022.AcceptanceCriteria: API entrypoint serves MCP/admin/portal surfaces without worker loops; worker entrypoint runs scheduling/ingestion/index jobs without exposing API routes

UC-023.Action: Opens `/admin/sites` to list, create, edit, delete, pause, and resume curated site sources with per-source index statistics
UC-023.Goal: Manage curated site source registry through admin UI without direct database access
UC-023.AcceptanceCriteria: Admin sites page lists all sources with per-source stats (page count, chunk count, last crawl); operator can create new sources with validated fields (source_id, name, domain, tier, language, focus, crawl_interval, max_pages); operator can edit existing source metadata; operator can pause/resume sources; operator can delete sources with full cascade (embeddings, chunks, pages, crawl jobs); all actions require valid admin session; page supports HTMX progressive enhancement

UC-024.Action: Applies layered per-source content cleanup (global rules + source adapter + quality gate) during crawl ingestion before chunking and embedding
UC-024.Goal: Remove domain-specific crawl noise (navigation, social widgets, newsletter promos, image placeholders) to improve chunk quality and retrieval relevance without changing MCP tool contracts
UC-024.AcceptanceCriteria: Cleanup pipeline runs deterministically and rule-based in parser stage; source adapters are selected by `source_id`; quality gate skips low-value pages as structured non-fatal outcomes with reason codes; `pages_skipped` counter is tracked in IngestionResult separately from `errors[]`; first adapter covers `wrenjapan`; existing MCP tool inputs/outputs remain unchanged

UC-025.Action: Routes tool calls to country-specific data sources using `country_code` parameter and per-country configuration from `country_settings` table
UC-025.Goal: Enable single MCP endpoint to serve multiple country destinations without per-country URL routing or multiple OAuth flows
UC-025.AcceptanceCriteria: Tools that accept `country_code` resolve per-country `tg_chat_ids` and site source filtering from `country_settings` row; `country_code` enum is built dynamically from countries with `status='active'`; adding a new country requires only DB inserts and server restart, no code changes

UC-026.Action: Manages country settings (status lifecycle: draft → active → coming_soon → maintenance) and per-country configuration via `country_settings` table
UC-026.Goal: Control country availability and per-country runtime configuration without code changes
UC-026.AcceptanceCriteria: `country_settings` table stores `country_code` (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 PK), `status`, and `settings` JSONB; status determines tool accessibility (`active` = tool calls allowed) and portal visibility; `settings` JSONB holds `tg_chat_ids` and extensible per-country config

UC-027.Action: Tracks per-user per-tool per-country MCP usage with `country_code` as part of the usage counter composite key
UC-027.Goal: Enable per-country usage analytics and future per-country pricing differentiation
UC-027.AcceptanceCriteria: `usage_counters` composite PK is `(user_id, tool_name, country_code)`; tools without `country_code` use default `'jp'` during migration; portal stats can be grouped by country

UC-201.Action: Builds offline retrieval evaluation pipeline (recall metrics / MRR / benchmark harness)
UC-201.Goal: Quantitatively optimize ranking and retrieval quality after v1 launch

UC-202.Action: Introduces advanced chunking/enrichment (LLM chunking, contextual retrieval, proposition indexing)
UC-202.Goal: Improve retrieval quality beyond deterministic v1 chunking baseline

UC-301.Goal: Hybrid multi-source retrieval tool in MCP boundary

UC-302.Action: Expose `validate_google_map_link` and `verify_tabelog` as MCP public tools
UC-302.Goal: Deterministic verification tools in MCP boundary

UC-303.Action: Expose unified MCP tool that merges message and site search into one API
UC-303.Goal: Simplify caller interface at cost of mixed retrieval semantics
